titleStmt.title: 26. Memorandum From Secretary of the Navy Forrestal to Secretary of War Patterson Source: Central Intelligence Agency Historical Files, HS/HC–31, Item 22. No classification marking. The source text is an extract transcribed in July 1952 for the CIA Historian. The original document has not been found.

div.head: 26. Memorandum From Secretary of the Navy Forrestal to Secretary of War Patterson Source: Central Intelligence Agency Historical Files, HS/HC–31, Item 22. No classification marking. The source text is an extract transcribed in July 1952 for the CIA Historian. The original document has not been found.
div.p: It occurs to me that these subjects which have common interest to both Departments might be the basis of a discussion between us some time in the near future:…

2. Joint Intelligence. The Joint Chiefs of Staff, as you know, made a recommendation to the President for a national intelligence organization, the general outline of which provides for intelligence supervision
by the War, State and Navy Departments, with a director charged with the working responsibility functioning under these individuals as a group. I think this is a subject which should have our close attention. The Joint Chiefs of Staff paper seems to me soundly conceived and, if you agree, I think we should push it vigorously at the White House.…

signed.note: Printed from a copy that indicates Forrestal signed the original.
